name: csvtools
goal: Build a Node.js CSV processing CLI with demo page, using texttools as architectural reference

steps.prompt:
Read /workflows/texttools.json — that file describes how a Node.js CLI was structured with package.json, src/index.js, and src/commands.js. Use the EXACT same architectural pattern to build a CSV processing CLI at /artifacts/csvtools/.

The CSV CLI must have these commands:
  count   — print row count and column count of a CSV from stdin
  headers — print the column names, one per line
  select  — filter to specific columns: echo data | node src/index.js select col1,col2
  filter  — keep rows where a column matches a value: node src/index.js filter name=Alice
  stats   — for each numeric column print min, max, avg

Follow the texttools pattern exactly:
  /artifacts/csvtools/package.json  — name csvtools, version 1.0.0, bin pointing to src/index.js
  /artifacts/csvtools/src/index.js  — arg parsing, stdin reading, dispatch to commands
  /artifacts/csvtools/src/commands.js — all five command implementations

The CLI reads CSV from stdin. All commands must handle quoted fields and commas inside quotes.
Write all three files.

Read /artifacts/csvtools/src/commands.js and /artifacts/csvtools/src/index.js to understand what was built.

Create a single self-contained HTML file at /artifacts/csvtools/index.html that:
- Embeds a sample CSV dataset (at least 10 rows, 5 columns — use realistic data like sales records)
- Has a live demo UI: user picks a command from a dropdown, enters optional args, clicks Run
- Executes the command logic IN THE BROWSER (port the JS commands.js logic inline)
- Shows output in a styled pre block
- Documents all 5 commands with examples

Embed all CSS and JavaScript inside the HTML file — no external files.
Make it visually polished with a dark code theme for output.

Verify the csvtools project is complete:
1. Read /artifacts/csvtools/src/index.js — confirm it handles all 5 commands
2. Read /artifacts/csvtools/src/commands.js — confirm parseCSV handles quoted fields
3. Read /artifacts/csvtools/index.html — confirm it has embedded CSS+JS and the live demo

If anything is missing or broken, fix it now.

Then emit: context.emit({ type: 'done', message: 'csvtools complete: CLI at artifacts/csvtools/src/, demo at artifacts/csvtools/index.html' })
